---
title: '20250825_Spp1_microglia'
output: html_document
date: "2025-08-25"
---

# 1.Dataset prep, gene prep
```{r, message= FALSE}
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)  # change to org.Mm.eg.db for mouse, org.Rn.eg.db for rat, etc.
library(enrichplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggrepel)

DEG_quarter <- read.csv("highSPP1_vs_lowSPP1_microglia_quarter_ALS_1015.csv", header = T, row.names = 1)
gene_up <- DEG_quarter %>%
  filter(avg_log2FC > 0.5 & p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  pull(gene)
gene_down <- DEG_quarter %>%
  filter(avg_log2FC < -0.5 & p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  pull(gene)
gene_up_entrez <- bitr(gene_up, fromType="SYMBOL", toType="ENTREZID", OrgDb=org.Hs.eg.db)
gene_down_entrez <- bitr(gene_down, fromType="SYMBOL", toType="ENTREZID", OrgDb=org.Hs.eg.db)

ego_up <- enrichGO(gene          = gene_up_entrez$ENTREZID,
                   OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
                   keyType       = "ENTREZID",
                   ont           = "BP",
                   pAdjustMethod = "BH",
                   pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
                   qvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
                   readable      = TRUE)
ego_up_simplified <- simplify(
  ego_up,
  cutoff = 0.7,       # similarity cutoff (0–1), lower = stricter
  by = "p.adjust",    # keep terms with best (lowest) adjusted p-value
  select_fun = min,   # function to select representative term
  measure = "Wang"    # semantic similarity measure ("Wang" is recommended)
)

ego_down <- enrichGO(gene          = gene_down_entrez$ENTREZID,
                     OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
                     keyType       = "ENTREZID",
                     ont           = "BP",
                     pAdjustMethod = "BH",
                     pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
                     qvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
                     readable      = TRUE)
ego_down_simplified <- simplify(
  ego_down,
  cutoff = 0.7,       # similarity cutoff (0–1), lower = stricter
  by = "p.adjust",    # keep terms with best (lowest) adjusted p-value
  select_fun = min,   # function to select representative term
  measure = "Wang"    # semantic similarity measure ("Wang" is recommended)
)

ekegg_up <- enrichKEGG(gene          = gene_up_entrez$ENTREZID,
                       organism      = 'hsa',   # change if not human
                       pvalueCutoff  = 0.05
                       )  %>% setReadable(OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, keyType = "ENTREZID")  # <-- convert after KEGG

ekegg_down <- enrichKEGG(gene          = gene_down_entrez$ENTREZID,
                         organism      = 'hsa',
                         pvalueCutoff  = 0.05
                         )  %>% setReadable(OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db, keyType = "ENTREZID")  # <-- convert after KEGG

write.csv(as.data.frame(ego_up), "260211ALS_Spp1HvsL_GO_up.csv", row.names=FALSE)
write.csv(as.data.frame(ego_down), "260211ALS_Spp1HvsL_GO_down.csv", row.names=FALSE)
write.csv(as.data.frame(ekegg_up), "260211ALS_Spp1HvsL_KEGG_up.csv", row.names=FALSE)
write.csv(as.data.frame(ekegg_down), "260211ALS_Spp1HvsL_KEGG_down.csv", row.names=FALSE)

# GO plots + KEGG plots
GOup <- dotplot(ego_up_simplified, showCategory = 8) +
  ggtitle("GO-Upregulated in SPP1 high microglia") +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8)
  )
GOup
GOdown <- dotplot(ego_down_simplified, showCategory = 8) +
  ggtitle("GO-Downregulated in SPP1 high microglia") +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8)
  )
GOdown
KEGGup <- dotplot(ekegg_up, showCategory = 8) +
  ggtitle("KEGG-Upregulated in SPP1 high microglia") +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8)
  )
KEGGup
KEGGdown <- dotplot(ekegg_down, showCategory = 8) +
  ggtitle("KEGG-Downregulated in SPP1 high microglia") +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8)
  )
KEGGdown
# Create output folder if it doesn't exist
outdir <- "GO_KEGG_TIFFs"
if(!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir)
# Save each plot as TIFF
ggsave(file.path(outdir, "GO_up.tiff"),
       plot = GOup, width = 7.5, height = 5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
ggsave(file.path(outdir, "GO_down.tiff"),
       plot = GOdown, width = 7.5, height = 5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
ggsave(file.path(outdir, "KEGG_up.tiff"),
       plot = KEGGup, width = 7.5, height = 5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
ggsave(file.path(outdir, "KEGG_down.tiff"),
       plot = KEGGdown, width = 7.5, height = 5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")

# Save using ggsave
{pdf("260211ALS_Spp1HvsL_GOKEGG_Enrichment_plots-DEG=0.5-n=8.pdf", width=7.5, height=5)
print(GOup)
print(GOdown)
print(KEGGup)
print(KEGGdown)
dev.off()
}

save(ego_up,ego_down,ekegg_up,ekegg_down,ego_up_simplified,ego_down_simplified, DEG_quarter,gene_up,gene_down, file='260211ALS_Spp1HvsL_GOKEGG-DEG=0.5.Rdata')   #Already normalized

```

# 2.Data filter
```{r, message= FALSE}
cnetplot(ego_up, showCategory=10, foldChange=NULL) +
  ggtitle("Gene–GO Term Network (Upregulated)")

cnetplot(ego_down, showCategory=10, foldChange=NULL) +
  ggtitle("Gene–GO Term Network (Downregulated)")




```


#----2.3 GSEA----
```{r, message= FALSE}
library(clusterProfiler)
library(enrichplot)
library(ggstatsplot)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(org.Mm.eg.db)


DEG_quarter <- read.csv("highSPP1_vs_lowSPP1_microglia_quarter_ALS_1015.csv", header = T, row.names = 1)
organism = 'hsa'    #  Human 'hsa' Mouse'mmu'   
OrgDb = 'org.Hs.eg.db'   #Human "org.Hs.eg.db" Mouse "org.Mm.eg.db"
need_DEG <- DEG_quarter[,c(2,5)] #choose "log2FoldChange" and "pvalue"）
need_DEG$SYMBOL <- rownames(need_DEG)
head(need_DEG)

#clusterProfiler
df <- bitr(rownames(need_DEG), 
           fromType = "SYMBOL",
           toType =  "ENTREZID",
           OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)  # Step 1: Convert SYMBOL to ENTREZID
df <- df[!duplicated(df$SYMBOL), ]  # Remove duplicates if any
need_DEG <- merge(need_DEG, df, by = "SYMBOL") # Step 2:merge symbol and entrezID
geneList <- need_DEG$avg_log2FC
names(geneList) <- need_DEG$ENTREZID
geneList <- sort(geneList, decreasing = T)  # Step 3: Prepare gene list for GSEA, sort in the decreasing manner
geneList 

#GSEA-KEGG: using  gseKEGG()
KEGG_kk_entrez <- gseKEGG(geneList     = geneList,
                          organism     = "hsa",
                          pvalueCutoff = 0.25)  
KEGG_kk <- DOSE::setReadable(KEGG_kk_entrez, 
                             OrgDb="org.Hs.eg.db",
                             keyType='ENTREZID')            
tmp_GSEA_KEGG=KEGG_kk@result
dim(tmp_GSEA_KEGG)

#GSEA-GO: using gseGO()
GO_kk_entrez <- gseGO(geneList     = geneList,
                      ont          = "ALL",  # "BP", "MF", "CC" or "ALL"
                      OrgDb        = "org.Hs.eg.db",
                      keyType      = "ENTREZID",
                      pvalueCutoff = 0.25)   
GO_kk_entrez_simplified <- clusterProfiler::simplify(GO_kk_entrez, cutoff=0.7, by="p.adjust", select_fun=min) #Usually it takes super long
GO_kk <- DOSE::setReadable(GO_kk_entrez_simplified, 
                           OrgDb="org.Hs.eg.db",
                           keyType='ENTREZID') 
tmp_GSEA_GO=GO_kk@result

save(KEGG_kk_entrez,GO_kk_entrez,GO_kk_entrez_simplified,tmp_GSEA_KEGG,tmp_GSEA_GO,file = "260211ALS_Spp1HvsL_GSEAresult.RData")
write.csv(tmp_GSEA_KEGG,file = '260211ALS_Spp1HvsL_GSEAresult_KEGG.csv')
write.csv(tmp_GSEA_GO,file = '260211ALS_Spp1HvsL_GSEAresult_GO.csv')

#gsea_matrix <- tmp_272626GO %>%
#  filter(str_detect(tmp_272626GO$Description,"extracellular matrix"))
#filtered_ID <- gsea_matrix[gsea_matrix$qvalue < 0.01, ]

#devtools::install_github("junjunlab/GseaVis")
#library(GseaVis)
#dotplotGsea (data=GO_kk_entrez_simplified, topn=5)


##Example: wanna combine the visualization for "matrix" 
dotplot_top8 <- dotplot(GO_kk_entrez_simplified, showCategory = 8, title = "GSEA Top 8 Enriched Terms")
dotplot_top8
ggsave(dotplot_top8, filename = "260211ALS_Spp1HvsL_GSEA-top8.tiff",width =6,height =4)

## ridgeplot for all
# Extract NES values from the GSEA result table
nes_df <- GO_kk_entrez_simplified@result %>%
    select(Description, NES)

ridgep_ALS <- ridgeplot(GO_kk_entrez_simplified,
                    showCategory = 8,
                    fill = "p.adjust",
                    label_format = 40, 
                    orderBy = "p.adjust",
                    decreasing = T)  +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8)
  )

ridgep_ALS +
    geom_text(
        data = nes_df,
        inherit.aes = FALSE,     # <-- prevents fill=p.adjust from being inherited
        aes(
            x = Inf,               # or Inf, depending on your plot
            y = Description,
            label = round(NES, 2)
        ),
        hjust = -0.2,
        size = 3
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    theme(plot.margin = margin(5.5, 40, 5.5, 5.5))


ridgep_ALS
ggsave(ridgep_ALS, filename = "260211ALS_Spp1HvsL_GSEA_ridgep.tiff",width =6,height =4.5)


 rm(list=ls())

```    


#----2.4 Bar plot of genes of interest----
```{r, message= FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)

DEG_quarter <- read.csv("highSPP1_vs_lowSPP1_microglia_quarter_ALS_1015.csv", header = T, row.names = 1)
GOI <- read.csv("Microglia_genelist.csv", header = F)
deg <- DEG_quarter
genes_of_interest <- trimws(GOI$V1)

#genes_of_interest <- c("SCARB3")

# Filter to your new gene list
df_plot <- deg %>%
  filter(gene %in% genes_of_interest) %>%
  mutate(
    neglog10_p = -log10(p_val_adj),
    gene = factor(gene, levels = rev(genes_of_interest))  # preserve your order
  )
df_plot$signif <- ifelse(df_plot$p_val_adj < 0.05, "*", "")

# Combined bar + color scale plot
ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = gene, y = avg_log2FC, fill = neglog10_p)) +
  geom_col(color = "black", width = 0.75) +
  geom_text(aes(label = signif), 
            hjust = -0.2, size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    name = "-log10(adj p)",
    low = "white" ,
    high ="#F7776E"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = " ",
    y = "Log2FC"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)+
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
  )+ theme(
  axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
  axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold")
)

ggsave("Genelist-SPP1_HvsL.tiff", dpi = 300, width = 4.5, height = 12, units = "in")
#rm(list=ls())
```    


## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
